nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: use it to draft things that are mostly repetitive but I can't just reuse existing things

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: Relying solely on own knowledge for unfamiliar topics
nodes.grounding_span: use AI to generate a list of suggested keywords and combinations

nodes.label: AI language appropriateness for audience
nodes.grounding_span: AI has a tendency to use language that's too academic for the students

nodes.label: Personal engagement with student problem-solving process
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use AI at all for student feedback

nodes.label: Ethical AI use in education
nodes.label_negative: AI use inconsistent with expectations for students
nodes.grounding_span: I expect students to not use AI to write their assignments wholesale... so I feel compelled to do my feedback without using AI

nodes.label: AI as tool for lesson planning
nodes.grounding_span: I'd rather teach students how to use it correctly instead of pretending we can control whether they do it or not

nodes.label: AI limitations in education and research
nodes.grounding_span: I don't believe excellent education or library reference and research can be provided completely by AI

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it's a timesaver to work from an imperfect list vs. do it perfectly myself

nodes.label: Pedagogical responsibility and role modeling
nodes.grounding_span: I expect students to not use AI to write their assignments wholesale... so I feel compelled to do my feedback without using AI

nodes.label: Student empowerment and leveling the playing field
nodes.grounding_span: it's a good teaching moment about using AI to enhance their research, not replace it

nodes.label: Preservation of human expertise in education
nodes.grounding_span: I don't believe excellent education or library reference and research can be provided completely by AI

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: It's satisfying because it's a good teaching moment about using AI to enhance their research

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use AI at all for student feedback

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: I don't believe excellent education or library reference and research can be provided completely by AI. But if it's cheaper... schools might do it anyway

nodes.label: Ethical consistency reasoning — aligning own AI use with expectations for students
nodes.grounding_span: I expect students to not use AI to write their assignments wholesale... so I feel compelled to do my feedback without using AI

nodes.label: Pragmatic adaptation to institutional constraints
nodes.grounding_span: if I'm working with an instructor of record who has a strict no-AI policy I have to adjust my instruction to that
